name: Repo Launch
description: Audit and polish a GitHub-ready repository. Use when Codex needs to classify a rough project, choose the right README shape, and add only the minimum launch essentials needed for a clean shareable repo.
short_description: Turn a rough project into a clean public repo.
audience: Solo builders preparing a prototype or rough folder for GitHub
Maintainers who need a compact README, license, and start path
Teams cleaning multi-project repos without adding documentation sprawl
detail_lines: Classifies the repository shape first, then adds only the README, license, gitignore, and start-path polish the repo actually needs.
Keeps launch cleanup grounded in existing files and runner commands instead of forcing a generic open-source template.
Works local-first on the selected checkout and avoids hidden registry, account, or marketplace behavior.
